## Epic 1 — Core Mechanics (#11)

**Dependency:** #10
**Est. Effort:** Small

### Goal
Fully design the six-attribute system and write it into chapter 03.

### Design Requirements
- Each attribute must have a clear, non-overlapping mechanical domain
- Range: -2 to +2 (0 = average human)
- Each attribute must govern at least 3 skills
- Each attribute must have a clear combat relevance
- Each attribute must have a clear non-combat relevance (social, exploration, crafting)

### Attributes
| Attribute | Domain | Governs |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| **Brawn (BR)** | Physical strength, melee power | Melee damage, carrying capacity, athletics |
| **Fortitude (FO)** | Endurance, resilience, health | HP calculation, poison/disease resistance |
| **Agility (AG)** | Speed, reflexes, coordination | Initiative, movement, dodging, ranged combat |
| **Guile (GU)** | Cunning, deception, social finesse | Social skills, stealth, bluffing |
| **Knowledge (KN)** | Education, lore, information recall | Knowledge skills, languages, identifying creatures |
| **Reason (RE)** | Logic, problem-solving, magical aptitude | Magic effectiveness, crafting, puzzle-solving |

### Tasks
- [ ] Define starting attribute determination method (point buy, array, or random)
- [ ] Define attribute modifier table (what does each score give as a bonus?)
- [ ] Define derived stats from attributes (HP, initiative, movement, etc.)
- [ ] Write chapter 03 — Attributes as proper AsciiDoc
- [ ] Include 2-3 narrative examples of each attribute in play
- [ ] Cross-reference to skills (ch07) and combat (ch13)
